chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI CP No.D-2542 of 2025 ____________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1. For hearing of CMA No.11878/2025 2. For hearing of main case 18.06.2025 Mr. Amjad Hayat, advocate for the petitioner Sardar Zafar Hussain, advocate for respondent No.2 Mr. Wajiha M. Mehdi, AAG --------- Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he would be satisfied if the instant petition is disposed of in terms of order dated 03.03.2025 passed in CP No.D-759 of 2025. Sardar Zafar Hussain, advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondent No.2 and states that he has no objection to such proposition. Order accordingly. For avoiding of doubt, the material part of order dated 03.03.2025 is set out below: “Learned counsel submits that the Petitioner is willing to deposit the undisputed quantum of duties and taxes and secure the differential amount of duties and taxes along with the adjudged fine and penalty. Per joint suggestion since the consignment is incurring the risk of diminution and storage charges at the port, let the undisputed quantum of duties and taxes be deposited with the Collectorate / Department and the disputed amount of the duties and taxes along with the adjudged fine and penalty be secured before the Nazir of this Court, by way of pay order(s) / ban guarantee in respect of the consignment's covered by this petition. Once the amount is secured as stipulated supra the Nazir shall issue the appropriate certificate/s If the amount is secured by way of pay order/s, the Nazir shall invest the amount in profit bearing instruments, as per the Rules The Nazir's fee is settled at Rs. 10,000/- per certificate.

Per joint suggestion since the consignment is incurring the risk of diminution and storage charges at the port, let the undisputed quantum of duties and taxes be deposited with the Collectorate / Department and the disputed amount of the duties and taxes along with the adjudged fine and penalty be secured before the Nazir of this Court, by way of pay order(s) / ban guarantee in respect of the consignment's covered by this petition. Once the amount is secured as stipulated supra the Nazir shall issue the appropriate certificate/s If the amount is secured by way of pay order/s, the Nazir shall invest the amount in profit bearing instruments, as per the Rules The Nazir's fee is settled at Rs. 10,000/- per certificate. Upon deposit of the undisputed quantum of duties and taxes with the Collectorate Department and presentation of the certificate/s issued by the Nazir the concerned Collectorate / Department shall release the consignment/s forthwith. The fate of the amount secured supra shall be subject to the outcome of the Appeal pending before the Collector of Customs (Appeals). This petition, alongwith pending application/s, is disposed of in terms herein.” The instant petition stands disposed of in the above terms alongwith pending application(s). Head of Const. Benches JUDGE Nadir*
